channel.title: World
channel.description: The Washington Post World section provides information and analysis of breaking world news stories. In addition to our world news and video, Post World News offers discussions and blogs on major international news and economic issues.

item.title: Letter written by Mary, Queen of Scots, prior to beheading draws crowds
item.dc:creator: Steve Hendrix
item.description: The letter, written in prison six hours before her execution, is on display in Scotland for the first time in 30 years, enthralling crowds and conspiracy theorists.

item.title: Khamenei’s son chosen as Iran’s supreme leader, extending hardline rule
item.dc:creator: Susannah George
item.description: The selection of Mojtaba Khamenei sent a message of defiance against Donald Trump after U.S.-Israeli strikes killed his father Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

item.title: 7th U.S. service member killed in war with Iran; Khamenei’s son named supreme leader
item.dc:creator: Susannah George, Sammy Westfall, Abbie Cheeseman, Dan Lamothe, Lior Soroka, Siham Shamalakh, Heba Farouk Mahfouz
item.description: The new supreme leader was selected as environmental fallout from Israeli strikes on fuel depots blanketed parts of Tehran.

item.title: Trump threat to blow up trade deal puts Canada’s auto town on the spot
item.dc:creator: David J. Lynch
item.description: The Canadian auto industry has been rocked by President Donald Trump’s abandonment of subsidies for electric vehicles and embrace of tariffs.

item.title: For Israel’s Netanyahu, Trump grants wishes, but his support carries risks
item.dc:creator: Steve Hendrix, John Hudson
item.description: Trump and Netanyahu, two political high rollers, are seen as more of an odd couple than Roosevelt-Churchill or Clinton-Blair. The war in Iran is their biggest gamble yet.

item.title: Trump said he ended a war in Africa. U.S. sanctions say otherwise.
item.dc:creator: Isaac Arnsdorf, Rael Ombuor
item.description: The administration says Rwandan-backed militants violated a Trump-brokered peace accord within days.

item.title: Trump says Iran targets will expand, honors U.S. troops killed in war
item.dc:creator: Maegan Vazquez, Victoria Craw, Lior Soroka, Suzan Haidamous, Mohamad El Chamaa, Siham Shamalakh, Heba Farouk Mahfouz
item.description: President Donald Trump honors slain U.S. service members at Dover, threatens to widen U.S. targets after Iran’s president dismissed the notion of surrender.

item.title: Laden Iranian ships depart Chinese port tied to key military chemicals
item.dc:creator: Joyce Lee, Meg Kelly
item.description: Experts said the vessels are probably carrying a key precursor for rocket fuel, making it notable that Beijing let them sail while the U.S. and Iran are at war.

item.title: Ukrainian forces halt advance in Zaporizhzhia, Kyiv officials say
item.dc:creator: Kostiantyn Khudov, David Stern
item.description: Ukraine has claimed a slew of successes on the front line in recent days, underscoring the effectiveness of its weapons systems, including anti-drone interceptors.

item.title: Trump team bashed Europe for a year. Now he wants support in war on Iran.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Francis, Michael Birnbaum
item.description: European leaders are ramping up their response to the crisis spreading outside Iran but remain wary of a conflict that could have untold ramifications.

item.title: Intel report warns large-scale war ‘unlikely’ to oust Iran’s regime
item.dc:creator: John Hudson, Warren Strobel
item.description: A classified U.S. report doubts that Iran’s opposition would take power following either a short or extended U.S. military campaign.

item.title: Why is the U.S. at war with Iran? Here’s what the Trump administration says.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Brown
item.description: President Donald Trump and top administration officials have offered a range of rationales for launching Operation Epic Fury.
